signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Policy Pivot
signals.text: NZD 1W momentum (+1.331%) vs CHF laggard status (-0.710%) creates a 2.041% G10 spread following the RBNZ's first rate hike in three years. The +250bp OIS carry advantage for NZD/CHF is reinforced by the RBNZ's hiking bias against the SNB's 0.00% hold. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 46% hike priced) on 2 September is the catalyst for confirming the extended tightening cycle.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Advantage vs Speculative Squeeze Risk
signals.text: JPY COT LF net short -104.2k (29.1% long) creates significant short-squeeze risk despite the +255bp OIS carry advantage favoring USD. While the bear steepener regime (2s10s +87.4bp) and strong US economic surprise index (+62) support USD, the extreme JPY short positioning leaves the pair vulnerable to any dovish shift in US CPI. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 July determines if the yield gap begins to compress.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Political Risk + Negative Data Momentum
signals.text: EUR/USD downside toward 1.1350 is driven by the death of Senator Lindsey Graham and potential US fiscal uncertainty alongside a negative EUR economic surprise index (-9). EUR COT net short -66.1k aligns with the +135bp OIS carry disadvantage to limit upside recovery. The next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 July is the catalyst for resolving the current hiking bias vs hold expectations.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT LF net long +24.1k (63.8% long) creates unwind risk as the currency faces a strong negative economic surprise index (-16). While the -82bp OIS carry advantage (favoring AUD) and RBA hold at 4.35% provide a floor, persistent data misses threaten the current 1W outperformance (+0.646%). The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 August determines if the neutral bias shifts to accommodative.

signals.title: EUR/GBP — Retail Sentiment Extreme vs Carry Neutrality
signals.text: Retail sentiment at 92% long on EUR/GBP signals a contrarian reversal risk toward the 0.8450 level. This positioning diverges from the moderate negative EUR data momentum (-9) and a near-neutral OIS carry environment. The next BoE meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 30 July serves as the catalyst for a directional breakout from the current range.
